job_title: Junior Executive-Finance & Admin - For SAJIDA FOUNDATION - Job ID: 1465371
job_description: SAJIDA FOUNDATION is looking for Junior Executive-Finance & AdminJob Description / Responsibilityপ্রতি মাসে পূর্বের মাসের কর্মীদের হাজিরা চেক করা জন্য সহযোগিতা করা।প্রতি মাসে সকল কর্মীদের ওভারটাইমশিট প্রস্তুত করার জন্য সহযোগিতা করা।কর্মী নিয়োগের জন্য প্রধান কাযার্লয়ের মাধ্যমে ইন্টারভিউর আয়োজন করা।নতুন কর্মীদের যোগদানপত্রের সকল কাগজপত্র তৈরী করে প্রধান কার্যালয়ে প্রেরণের ব্যবস্থা গ্রহণ করা ।অব্যহতি/পদত্যাগ কর্মীর সকল কাগজপত্র তৈরী করে প্রধান কার্যালয়ে প্রেরণ করা হয়।কর্মীদের অনিয়ম সংঘটিত কারণ দশার্নোর নোটিশ, পরামর্শপত্র ইত্যাদি প্রস্তত করা হয়।এইচ.আর. সংক্রান্ত সকল ফাইল, চিঠি এবং নীতিমালা ইত্যাদি সংরক্ষণ করা ।যাতায়াত বিল পর্যবেক্ষণ ও শিট প্রস্তুত করা।সকল কর্মীদের স্বাস্থ্যবীমার সংক্রান্ত সকল কাগজপত্র তৈরী করে প্রধান কাযার্লয়ে প্রেরণ করা হয়।প্রশিক্ষণ ও মিটিং আয়োজনে সহযোগিতা করা।প্রতিদিন সকালে হাসপাতালের ও ফার্মেসী থেকে ক্যাশ টাকা গ্রহণ এবং নির্দিষ্ট সময়ের মধ্যে ব্যাংকিং কাজ সমাপ্ত করা।প্রতিদিন হাসপাতাল ও ফার্মেসীর পৃথক ভাবে ভাউচার, ক্যাশ ও ব্যাংক বুক, লেজারসহ সকল তথ্য সফটওয়্যারে আপডেট করার জন্য সহযোগিতা করা।যে কোন বিল প্রদানের পূর্বে নীতিমালা অনুযায়ী সকল প্রক্রিয়া সম্পন্ন করে ভ্যাট ও ট্যাক্স কর্তন করা ও সময়মত সরকারী কোষাগারে জমা প্রদান করা এবং তার কপি স্থানীয় ভ্যাট অফিসে জমা প্রদান করা।হিসাব বিভাগের লেনদেনের তথ্যাদি ও বিল ভাউচার, নোটিশ, এগ্রিমেন্ট ও প্রয়োজনীয় সকল ফাইল নিরাপদে সংরক্ষণ করা, আভ্যন্তরীন ও বহিঃ নিরিক্ষা কাজে প্রয়োজনীয় সহযোগীতা করা।সুপারভাইজার কে তথ্য প্রদান করা।হাসপাতালের যে কোনো মিটিং এবং ট্রেনিং এ অংশগ্রহণ করা।Educational RequirementsBachelor of Commerce (BCom) in AccountingBachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in AccountingBachelor of Business Studies (BBS) in AccountingSkills Required: Financial Analysis, Microsoft ExcelExperience RequirementsAt least 2 year(s)The applicants should have experience in the following business area(s):NGOJob LocationDhaka (Keraniganj)

job_title: Officer, Finance - For JAAGO Foundation Trust - Job ID: 1465911
job_description: JAAGO Foundation Trust is looking for Officer, FinanceJob Description / ResponsibilityBrief description of JAAGO Foundation Trust: JAAGO Foundation Trust is a non-profit development organization with a mission to eradicate poverty in Bangladesh through education and positive change. JAAGO focuses on various sectors, including education, youth development, women empowerment, climate change, and governance. JAAGO provides free, high-quality education to children through conventional and digital schooling programs, serving 4500 students currently. Our Youth Development Program empowers 50,000 young citizens with skills and resources for national development. JAAGO addresses challenges faced by women and girls, offering alternative learning opportunities in underrepresented communities. JAAGO actively collaborates with local communities, women, youth, and marginalized groups to develop climate resilience and sustainable livelihood options. JAAGO promotes civic education to enhance youth civic participation, collective action, and leadership for effective societal change. Position Summary:The Officer, Finance is responsible for supporting day-to-day financial activities of JAAGO Foundation Trust, ensuring accurate accounting, proper documentation, compliant payment processing, and adherence to statutory and organizational policies. The role ensures smooth financial transactions and maintains strong internal control systems. Also, this role involves handling financial tracking, reporting, procurement, and logistics support. This position will work with a diverse team to maintain accurate records and assist in organizing project activities, ensuring the smooth operational flow of the assigned projects.Job Responsibilities:Accounting & Software Entry:Enter financial transactions into accounting software accurately and timely.Ensure correct account coding and project budget allocation.Maintain updated books of accounts.Assist in monthly closing and reconciliation processes. Voucher Preparation & Payment Processing:Prepare payment, journal, and adjustment vouchers with proper supporting documents.Verify bills, invoices, approvals, and budget availability before processing.Process payments (bank/cash/online) as per organizational policy.Maintain payment registers and tracking sheets.Ensure timely settlement of advances and proper adjustments. Documentation, Filing & Archiving:Maintain proper filing of vouchers, contracts, invoices, and financial documents.Ensure systematic archiving (both physical and digital).Maintain document confidentiality and secure record management.Prepare financial documents for internal and external audits. Banking Activities:Prepare cheques and online transfer documents.Coordinate with banks for deposits, fund transfers, and account-related issues.Collect bank statements and assist in preparing Bank Reconciliation Reports (BRR).Monitor bank balances and report discrepancies. VAT & Tax Compliance:Deduct VAT & Tax at source as per Bangladesh government rules.Prepare VAT & Tax challans and ensure timely deposit to the government treasury.Maintain VAT & Tax registers and related documentation.Support preparation of statutory compliance reports. Compliance & Internal Control:Ensure compliance with organizational financial policies and procedures.Support procurement compliance from a financial perspective.Assist during internal and external audits.Identify and report discrepancies or irregularities to the supervisor.Procurement & Logistics:Oversee procurement activities, ensuring all purchases and services are aligned with project objectives and donor policies.Coordinate procurement processes, including sourcing vendors, obtaining quotes, and securing the best value for money.Manage logistics for project-related events, including organizing venues, travel arrangements, and resources for training sessions, workshops, and community outreach activities.Compliance & Audit:Ensure compliance with donor regulations, financial reporting requirements, and audit processes.Work with internal and external auditors to ensure project financials are audit-ready and meet donor guidelines.Implement corrective actions as needed based on audit feedback to maintain financial transparency.Reporting Support:Assist in preparation of monthly financial reports.Provide financial data to project teams when required.Support budget monitoring activities. Safeguarding:Uphold and promote the organization’s safeguarding policies to ensure the safety, dignity, and well-being of all children, young people, and vulnerable adults engaged in our activities.Immediately report any concerns, suspicions, or incidents of safeguarding breaches in line with the organization’s reporting procedures.Maintain professional boundaries and conduct that fosters a safe and respectful environment for all beneficiaries, colleagues, and stakeholders.Educational RequirementsBachelor of Commerce (BCom) in AccountingBachelor’s or Master’s degree in Financ

Chief Financial Officer (CFO) - IPO-Focused | Real Estate | Hospitality | Infrastructure | Wellness | Private Equity.Location: Nanakaramguda, Hyderabad (On-site).Company: Ridhira Group - Building the World's Largest Wellness Communities.About Ridhira Group.Ridhira Group is a purpose-driven, next-generation conglomerate at the intersection of real estate, five-star hospitality, infrastructure, and wellness.We are on a mission to reurbanize 100 million Indians through wellness-centric resort living communities, integrating:.Wellness Real Estate & Sustainable Townships.Five-Star Resorts & Boutique Hospitality.Spa & Wellness (Ode Spa, Ode Life).Preventive Diagnostics & HealthTech.Serviced Villas & Investment-Grade Plots.Subscription Wellness & Timeshare Clubs.Flagship projects like Ridhira Zen, Ridhira Amara, and Flamingo Coast are redefining preventive health tourism and luxury sustainable living in India.We are now preparing for a public listing (IPO) in the next 24-36 months and seeking a visionary CFO who has already done it before - scaling high CAPEX businesses, raising capital globally, and building financial structures that endure for decades.Your Mission As CFO As our Chief Financial Officer, you will be the architect of our financial future, working directly with the Founder-CEO and the Board to:. Drive capital raising from private equity, family offices, banks, NBFCs, REITs, sovereign wealth funds, and strategic investors. Lead financial strategy for a multi-vertical business spanning real estate, hospitality, wellness, and infrastructure. Shape the IPO-readiness roadmap and ensure governance, compliance, and investor confidence at global standards. Design PropCo/OpCo, SPV, and fractional ownership structures to unlock value across projects. Orchestrate M&A deals, hospitality acquisitions, and underperforming resort takeovers. Build a world-class finance team and ERP-enabled financial control ecosystem.Key ResponsibilitiesStrategic Finance & Capital Structuring: Raise $50M-$500M through equity, debt, and hybrid instruments. Develop REIT-ready and AIF-friendly investment frameworks. Align capital allocation to a pan-India and global expansion roadmap. Create investor-friendly IRR and waterfall models for large-scale projects.Investor Relations & M&A Represent Ridhira Group at international investment forums & roadshows. Build long-term relationships with Brookfield, Blackstone, GIC, KKR, CPPIB, ADIA, and other global capital leaders. Identify & execute acquisitions in hospitality, wellness, and high-value land banks.Financial Operations & IPO Preparation Lead IPO documentation, governance compliance, and SEBI readiness. Oversee audits, tax strategy, cross-border compliance, and financial consolidation across 25+ entities. Implement advanced ERP, FP&A, and cost control systems.Ideal Background 15-25 years in finance leadership, with at least 5 years as CFO in real estate, hospitality, infrastructure, or PE-backed ventures. Proven success in IPO execution, large-scale capital raises, and M&A integrations. Strong network with banks, PEs, family offices, and sovereign wealth funds. CA / CFA / CPA / MBA from ISB, IIMs, INSEAD, Wharton, or equivalent.Preferred Industry ExperienceReal Estate & Infra: DLF, Embassy, Lodha, Godrej, L&T Realty, Shapoorji, Prestige, Hiranandani, Raheja, Tata Realty, Adani Realty, GMR.Hospitality & Leisure: IHCL (Taj), Marriott, Oberoi, Hyatt, ITC, Club Mahindra, Sterling.Wellness & Healthcare: Apollo, CureFit, Manipal, VLCC, Jiva, Kaya.Financial Services & PE: Blackstone, Brookfield, Kotak Infra, IIFL, Edelweiss, Motilal Oswal.Why Join Ridhira Group.Lead one of India's most ambitious IPO journeys in the next 24-36 months.Shape the future of wellness-oriented real estate at national scale.Work alongside visionary founders, architects, and global hospitality pioneers.Receive industry-best compensation, ESOPs, and global exposure.(ref:iimjobs.com)

Job Responsibilities: - Lead the Finance Department, overseeing all accounting functions, including Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), and General Ledger (GL) operations. Manage monthly account closings, prepare annual statutory financial statements, conduct audits, and file various tax returns. Prepare comprehensive monthly management reports that analyze business performance against budget and prior year results. Provide actionable insights to management to enhance financial performance and drive excellence. Identify opportunities for cost reduction and operational improvements across the organization. Collaborate with departmental managers to address instances where expenses exceed approved budgets, working together to develop strategies to bridge financial gaps. Enhance and manage working capital health, focusing on AP and AR aging in alignment with financial Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). Oversee and optimize monthly liquidity management, cash flow forecasting, and cash position, ensuring timely fulfillment of payment obligations, including loan and interest repayments, in accordance with company policies and KPIs. Ensure ongoing compliance with relevant laws and regulations, including tax reporting, statutory audit requirements, and annual filings. Partner with the Legal team to secure necessary business permits, licenses, insurance, and clearances by preparing and submitting required documentation and fees to government agencies and regulatory bodies. Maintain robust financial controls, including reconciliations and proper audit trails, in line with company policies. Ensure that all projects and financial deliverables are completed within established deadlines. Utilize hands-on experience with financial software such as NetSuite, Tally, and ERP systems. Perform additional tasks as assigned by the Group CFO.Qualifications Semi-qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) preferred; CA qualified candidates need not apply.

proven ability to manage Assets Under Management (AUM) of ₹1,500 Crores or above.Technical Expertise: Strong accounting knowledge; expertise in financial accounts, fundraising, RBI compliance, treasury, ALM, and stakeholder management.Soft Skills: Strategic, analytical, and governance-oriented; Company DescriptionContinental develops pioneering technologies and services for sustainable and connected mobility of people and their goods. Founded in 1871, the technology company offers safe, efficient, intelligent and affordable solutions for vehicles, machines, traffic and transportation. With its premium portfolio in the car, truck, bus, two-wheel and specialty tire segment, the Tires group sector stands for innovative solutions in tire technology. job_title: Finance Manager, Asia Global Selling Finance, ISS Finance, WW Global Selling Finance
job_description: DescriptionMore than 300 million customers shop in Amazon’s store, and every day, customers browse, purchase, and review products sold by third-party sellers right alongside products sold by Amazon. Since 2000, Amazon has offered this digital shelf space to individuals and companies of all sizes, helping them reach hundreds of millions of customers, build their brands, and grow their business. Fast-forward to the present day, and these third-party seller products account for more than half of all units sold in our store. The Amazon Global Selling team is responsible for recruiting 3P sellers and helping them grow their business on Amazon, either through their own logistic networks or leveraging Amazon's global fulfillment and customer service network. We place great focus on building a healthy Seller business which determines the future and potential of Amazon's 3P business.Amazon Global Selling Finance is seeking a Finance Manager to support the Asia Global Selling business. Finance is critical to the success in our mission. We see the entire picture, and are a cornerstone to driving the right investments, maintaining controllership, and distilling down the right stories to push our business forward.The role will support the day-to-day financial reporting and planning, annual operating plans and quarterly guidance, weekly business reviews at the inputs grain, and month-end close stories.Key job responsibilities Use solid financial knowledge and business acumen to build financial models, interpret data, draw conclusions, and make recommendations that deliver results, drive improvements, and influence leaders throughout Amazon. These decisions can have global impact. Educate business partners on financial and non-financial trade-offs and provides guidance and financial analysis for new projects and initiatives. You make thoughtful judgement calls in ambiguous environments where data may be scarce or difficult to procure. Identifies opportunities to resolve defects proactively. Improves, scales, or simplifies finance tools across teams and organizations.Basic Qualifications Bachelor's degree in BI, finance, engineering, statistics, computer science, mathematics, finance or equivalent quantitative field 7+ years of finance or a related analytical field experience 5+ years of applying key financial performance indicators (KPIs) to analyses experience 5+ years of building financial and operational reports/data sets that inform business decision-making experience 4+ years of controllership or compliance experience Experience delivering forecasting, budgeting, and variance analysis, and data interpretation of results Experience building and managing financial models for business forecasting and problem solvingPreferred Qualifications 7+ years of solving complex business challenges by delivering accurate and timely financial models, analysis, and recommendations that have a proven impact on business (e.g., financial savings, operational improvements, or customer benefits) experience Knowledge of SQL/ETL Experience working with large-scale data mining and reporting tools (examples: SQL, MS Access, Essbase, Cognos) and other financial systems (examples: Oracle, SAP, Lawson, JD Edwards) MBA, or CPAOur inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. Who We AreAt Brooks, we believe movement is the key to feeling more alive. That’s why we’re driven to create gear and experiences that take people to the place that makes them feel more alive — whether it’s a headspace, a feeling, or a finish line.Everyone who works at Brooks is propelled by a company culture that sparks excitement, fuels collaboration, inspires creativity, and ignites innovation.Our brand values help bond us together and drive our success: Runner First We act in the best interest of the runner Word is Bond We do what we say we’ll do Champion Heart We give our all in everything we do There is no “I” in Run We stay generous with our humanity Keep Moving We find ways to move every day, because joy is kinetic! We welcome everyone from every walk of life looking to inspire others through the power of movement — because we’re all moving towards something. Let’s run there.Your JobThe Senior Financial Analyst will play a key role in supporting the company’s growth. In the initial phase, this role will focus on supporting the setup and stabilization of core finance operations, particularly Procure-to-Pay (P2P), Accounts Payable processes and systems, while gaining a deep understanding of the cost structure and operational drivers of the business. As the finance function matures, the role will progressively expand into FP&A ownership, supporting business planning and performance management activities across the organization. The role will also contribute to special projects, acting as a thought partner to translate financial data into insights that inform resource allocation, efficiency initiatives, and long-term growth decisions.Your ResponsibilitiesOwn the administration and management of Medius, our Accounts Payable system, for APAC including management of master data and ensuring data accuracy and compliance with internal controls.Contribute to the design, documentation, training, and continuous improvement of Procure-to-Pay (P2P) policies and procedures, including Accounts Payable and employee travel & expense (T&E) processes. Ensure compliance with the 3-way match process, including validation of approved PO/SOW, goods or service confirmation, invoice accuracy, and adherence to contract terms and payment approval matrices. Identify and remediate any issues by collaborating with internal stakeholders to ensure timely resolution of any flagged items. Implement initial Accounts Payable (AP) set-up for APAC; manage ongoing Accounts Payable operations by partnering with internal stakeholders and external service providers.Own employee travel and expense administration through Concur, including expense report review, policy compliance checks, and assist with reimbursement processing.Progressively take on FP&A responsibilities, including budgeting support, rolling forecasts, variance analysis, and preparation of management reports.Partner with functional stakeholders to analyze cost drivers, performance trends, and financial impacts, translating operational data into actionable insights.Prepare monthly performance analysis and ad-hoc financial studies, highlighting key risks, opportunities, and drivers for management decision-making.Lead or participate in special projects and cross-functional initiatives, contributing financial analysis and insights to improve operational efficiency and support sustainable business growth.Contribute to finance process optimization, automation, and continuous improvement initiatives to support a fast-growing and evolving organization.Collaborate closely with the Finance Director on improving forecasting accuracy, reporting standards, data visualization, and the quality of insights provided to business leadership.QualificationsBachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or a related field; CPA, CMA, ACCA, or MBA preferred.5+ years of relevant accounting and finance experience, with solid exposure to Accounts Payable and FP&A responsibilities.Familiarity with Procure-to-Pay (P2P) processes, Accounts Payable, and employee expense reimbursement. Experience with Medius, Concur, and Infor M3 is a plus.Big Four or professional services background is a plus.Fluent in both Mandarin and English (written and spoken).Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to work in a multinational, fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment, anticipate issues, and resolve problems in a timely manner.Exposure to retail or consumer goods industries is a plus.High attention to detail, strong execution capability, and a proactive, self-driven working style.Strong teammate, successful collaborator, ability to influence, motivate and mentor others.Strong analytical skills and attention to detail without losing sight of the big picture. Able to analyze raw data, draw conclusions, and develop actionable strategic recommendations.Strong business acumen with the ability to translate business strategy or initiatives into financial models and/or in-depth analysisStrong proficiency in Excel; Workday Ad

Fanatics Collectibles is seeking a strategic, analytical, and business-savvy finance professional to join our growing international finance team. job_description: 工作职责:The Senior Financial Analyst – Regional FP&A role sits within the Regional FP&A team and plays a dual mission: act as a Finance Business Partner (BP) to support a small division with strong commercial and P&L understandingand serve as a digital champion within the region, actively driving and participating in finance digitalization and process optimization initiatives.This role offers strong exposure across functions and businesses, providing a total company view and a solid platform for future career growth within FP&A, commercial finance, or transformation roles.This role is ideal for a high-potential FP&A professional who enjoys combining business partnering with digital innovation and wants to grow within a dynamic regional finance organization.What You Will Do Finance Business Partnering (Division Support)Act as the primary Finance BP for a designated small division, partnering closely with business leaders to support decision-making.Develop a solid understanding of the business model, key commercial levers, margin drivers, and SG&A components.Perform monthly actual vs. forecast analysis, identify risks and opportunities, and provide clear, actionable insights.Support rolling forecast, annual budget, and strategic planning processes in collaboration with regional and division stakeholders.Provide analysis on topline, gross margin, cost structure, and operating income, linking financial outcomes to business drivers. Digitalization & Process Optimization (Digital Champion)Act as a digital ambassador within the Regional FP&A team, proactively identifying opportunities to improve efficiency, accuracy, and insight quality.Participate in and support finance digitalization projects, including reporting automation, data visualization, and planning tool enhancements.Leverage tools such as Excel, SAP, Power BI, and emerging digital / AI-enabled solutions to streamline FP&A processes.Continuously challenge existing processes and drive process simplification, standardization, and optimization.Demonstrate curiosity and passion for AI, digital finance, and new ways of working, with the ability to learn quickly and apply ideas pragmatically. Other Regional Ad-hoc tasksWill have opportunities to take on various ad-hoc tasks or projects任职条件:Education & ExperienceBachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Management.Typically 3–5 years of experience in FP&A, management reporting, or commercial finance, preferably in a multinational or matrix environment.Hands-on experience in business partnering and performance management.Technical & Analytical SkillsStrong understanding of P&L mechanics, including revenue, margin, and SG&A drivers.Solid financial modeling, analytical, and problem-solving skills; strong Excel capability is required.Experience with ERP systems (e.g. SAP) and data / visualization tools such as Power BI is a strong plus.Interest or exposure to digital, automation, and AI applications in finance is highly desirable.Soft Skills & MindsetAgile, fast learner with the ability to adapt quickly in a changing environment.Strong ownership mindset with a proactive, hands-on approach.Effective communicator who can translate complex financial information into clear business messages.Collaborative team player, comfortable working across functions and managing constructive challenges.Passion for continuous improvement, digitalization, and process optimization.Why Join the Regional FP&A TeamGain a total company and regional perspective, beyond a single business or function.Work closely with senior finance and business leaders across divisions.Be part of a team that values agility, digital innovation, and continuous improvement.Exposure to diverse topics including performance management, transformation projects, and strategic initiatives.A strong platform for long-term career development within FP&A, commercial finance, or finance transformation roles.

Are you an experienced finance leader who thrives in a fast‑paced, dynamic environment?Do you excel at driving accounting excellence, operational rigor, and financial governance?Are you looking to make a strategic impact within a global technology and services organization? SoftwareOne’s Finance team is looking for a highly seasoned Senior Finance Manager to lead our China Finance function. This role spans accounting operations, financial reporting, compliance, internal controls, and business partnering. You will oversee end‑to‑end finance operations, manage a local finance team, and play a key role in strengthening our financial governance framework. Key responsibilities: Lead and manage all Accounting Operations for China, including AR, AP, GL, and Tax.Oversee and coach the local Finance team, ensuring strong operational discipline and capability development.Drive month-end closing activities (journal entries, GL reviews, balance sheet reconciliations) in compliance with statutory, tax, and group reporting requirements.Support the Head of Finance in preparing monthly/quarterly management reporting for local and APAC stakeholders.Manage cash flow, liquidity, and working capital to ensure financial stability and optimize operational needs.Coordinate external audits, tax filings, and regulatory submissions with auditors and advisors.Lead continuous process improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and internal controls. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree or above in Finance, Accounting, or related fields; CPA / ACCA highly preferred. Diversified finance experience (accounting, internal controls, FP&A, or hybrid roles). Demonstrated success in leading and developing finance teams in a multinational environment. A strong team-first attitude with agility to support diverse finance functions; embraces a growth mindset and continuous improvement culture. Excellent communication skills with strong English proficiency. Advanced proficiency in Excel, BI tools, and ERP systems (e.g., NAV). Strong resilience and ability to perform in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment. Company description SoftwareOne and Crayon have come together to form a global, AI-powered software and cloud solutions provider with a bold vision for the future. With a footprint in over 70 countries and a diverse team of 13,000+ professionals, we offer unparalleled opportunities for talent to grow, make an impact, and shape the future of technology. At the heart of our business is our people. We empower our teams to work across borders, innovate fearlessly, and continuously develop their skills through world-class learning and development programs. Whether you're passionate about cloud, software, data, AI, or building meaningful client relationships, you’ll find a place to thrive here. Join us and be part of a purpose-driven culture where your ideas matter, your growth is supported, and your career can go global.

About the Role:We are looking for a proactive and commercially savvy Finance Business Partner to join our team in the [Durables/Consumer Electronics] sector. In this role, you will move beyond traditional reporting to act as a co-pilot to the Sales and Marketing leadership. Your primary mission will be to drive top-line growth while ensuring profitability through rigorous channel performance management, pricing strategy, and trade spend optimization.Key Responsibilities:1. Commercial Partnership & Top-line Growth· Partner closely with the Sales and Marketing teams to develop strategies that drive revenue growth across all product categories.· Challenge and support the business to identify new revenue streams and market opportunities.· Lead the monthly sales forecasting process, ensuring alignment with market trends, inventory levels, and promotional calendars.2. Channel Performance Management· Analyze profitability across different sales channels (e.g., Retail, E-commerce, Distributors, B2B, Direct Sales).· Develop KPIs to monitor channel health, sell-through rates, and inventory turnover.· Provide insights on channel mix optimization to maximize market share and EBITDA.3. Pricing & Trade Spend Analysis· Lead pricing analysis for new product launches and ongoing portfolio management. Model the financial impact of price elasticity and competitive pricing moves.· Manage the Trade Spend/Incentives budget. Partner with sales to ensure ROI on promotions and customer discounts.· Conduct post-mortem analysis on major sales events to evaluate effectiveness.4. Rebate & Incentive Management· Oversee the calculation, accrual, and settlement of complex customer and distributor rebate programs.· Ensure rebate structures are designed to incentivize desired customer behaviors (volume growth, category share gain) while protecting margins.· Audit and validate rebate claims to prevent revenue leakage.5. Strategic Planning & Control· Lead the development of the Annual Operating Plan (Budget) and Long-Range Plan (LRP) for the business unit.· Ensure compliance with internal controls related to revenue recognition and sales deductions.Qualifications & Requirements:· Education: Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or Economics. MBA or professional qualification (CPA/ACCA/CIMA) is a plus.· Experience: 5+ years of experience in financial planning and analysis (FP&A) or commercial finance, preferably within the Consumer Durables, Retail, or FMCG industries.· Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Excel and experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle). Proficiency in BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) for data visualization is highly preferred.· Commercial Acumen: Strong understanding of P&L drivers, with specific expertise in revenue recognition, gross margin analysis, and sales channel economics.· Soft Skills: Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills; ability to translate complex financial data into actionable business insights for non-finance teams.By clicking 'apply', you give your express consent that Robert Half may use your personal information to process your job application and to contact you from time to time for future employment opportunities. job_description: ASSOCIATION FOR ALTERNATIVE DEVELOPMENT (AFAD) is looking for Finance And Admin OfficerJob Description / ResponsibilityProject: Renewed Women’s Voice and Leadership (RWVL) BangladeshReports to: Project Manager (PM)Position Level: Mid LevelProject BriefThe Renewed Women’s Voice and Leadership (RWVL) project aims to improve women's and girls' access to human rights in all their diversity and to advance gender equality in Bangladesh, led by Manusher Jonno Foundation (MJF) and funded by Global Affairs Canada (GAC). This project will focus on empowering diverse voices and strengthening collective actions to advance women’s human rights by addressing gender-based violence (GBV), sexual and reproductive health & rights (SRHR), inclusive economic empowerment (iEE), and the safety and security of project participants.As an implementing partner of MJF, Association for Alternative Development (AFAD) will carry out the project activities at the grassroots level in the Kurigram Sadar upazilas within the Kurigram district. This project will focus on issues categorized across various pillars. Pillar 1: Will support organizational capacity and sustainability in areas such as (i) governance improvement; (ii) financial system development; and (iii) policy articulation. Pillar 2: Will contribute to gender equality programming and advocacy at the grassroots level through (i) supporting agency creation, (ii) promoting women’s leadership, (iii) providing knowledge and skill building, to challenge social norms and practices that oppose women’s rights. (iv) implementing social campaigns and mass awareness events, and (v) facilitating inclusive economic empowerment and entrepreneurship interventions. Pillar 3: Will focus on movement strengthening through (i) local network and alliance creation for collective advocacy, (ii) collective movement building.Job ProfileThe Finance and Admin Officer will have a dual-purpose function that combines support for operations, logistics, and human resources with financial management.To ensure smooth project implementation, Finance and Admin Officer will be responsible to manage day-to-day financial operations that includes cash flow, bookkeeping, budgeting, payroll, reporting, Tax compliances, while ensuring compliance with donor regulations and internal policies.She/he also accomplishes day-to-day administrative tasks that include logistics arrangement, procurement, recruitment process, and all process documents preservation, etc. She/he will maintain financial records, support audits, manage office inventory, and provide logistical support to staff.Working ConditionsThe position is based in Kurigram District, and need based field visits to the working areas are required.Job Summary:Financial Management and ComplianceMaintain cashbooks and ledgers, process payments, ensure necessary supporting documents with vouchers, manage staff advances, petty cash.Ensuring all expenditures comply with specific donor agreement and internal organizational policies.Ensure VAT/Tax compliance as per government rule.Maintain all necessary register related to accounts.Meet all the auditing requirements both external and internal.BudgetingPrepare project and periodical budget; monitoring expenditure rates and prepare variances report ensure funds are utilized appropriately.Financial ReportingPrepare financial reports to meet the donor reporting requirements including necessary statements (Bank Reconciliation, call forward, Advance list).Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports as per donor requirements.Administrative and Operational OperationsManage office supplies, equipment inventory, and fixed asset registers and stock register.Assist procurement committee to comply the policy.Organize logistical arrangements (travel, accommodation, workshops, trainings, networking).Ensure that the daily operational needs of the office are met.Human ResourcesMaintain staff contracts, timesheets, and leave records.Maintaining staff personnel files, recruitment related documents, and attendance records.Upholding policies on gender equality, child protection, and prevention of sexual exploitation and abuse (PSEAH).Financial MonitoringFinancial visit to project area, spot checks for fact-based findings (Need based).Capacity Building and iEEE financial ManagementProviding financial training and guidance to community-based organizations (CBOs), women groups, and beneficiaries. Supporting Community-Based Organizations (CBOs) or women’s groups in maintaining their own financial documents and bookkeeping for inclusive economic empowerment and entrepreneurship (iEEE) interventions and fund disbursement.Prepare accurate financial reports for iEEE interventionsAOBAny other task related to accountsEducational RequirementsBachelor of Business Administration (BBA)Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in AccountingBachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration.Experience Requirements3 to 4 year(s)Additional

OverviewFOBO businesses in Europe, AMESA and APAC have migrated its planning capability from XLS to MOSAIC, an integrated and digital planning tool, in a step forward towards reaching the Financial Planning 2025 Vision. However, the underlaying FOBO operating model limits our ability to capture benefits given the high attrition and lack of process standardization.To become more capable, agile, and efficient a fundamental change in the way we do FOBO Financial Planning is required, which will be addressed by establishing the FOBO Planning Central (FPC).FPC evolves the GBS approach, pivoting from a geography focus to a process focus, and allows BUs to concentrate their attention on the Bottlers. OverviewFOBO businesses in Europe, AMESA and APAC have migrated its planning capability from XLS to MOSAIC, an integrated and digital planning tool, in a step forward towards reaching the Financial Planning 2025 Vision. Planning services will be provided by single team, based in HBS, led by a single leader to serve FOBO globally. About the role:The Manager/Senior Manager – FP&A will lead the financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance management for the organization. The role will drive strategic decision-making by delivering accurate financial insights, variance analysis, business performance dashboards, and management reporting. The individual will partner with business leaders to optimize costs, improve profitability, and support growth initiatives. Key Responsibilities 1. Budgeting & Forecasting Lead the annual budgeting process and periodic forecasting cycles (monthly/quarterly). 2. Financial Reporting & MIS Prepare monthly management reports, dashboards, and key financial insights for leadership. Analyse P&L, Balance Sheet, and cashflow trends to identify risks and opportunities. Deliver variance analysis (Actual vs Budget vs Forecast) and recommend corrective actions. Support preparation of board/leadership presentations with clear storytelling. 3. Business Partnering Act as a finance business partner to operational teams and department heads. 4. Cost & Performance Management Drive cost tracking and identify opportunities for efficiency improvement. 5. Financial Modelling & Strategic Analysis Build and maintain robust financial models for scenario planning and sensitivity analysis. Support long-term financial planning (3-year / 5-year business plan). Provide insights on pricing strategies, revenue recognition impact, and financial risk assessment. 6. Process Improvement & Automation Improve FP&A processes through standardization and automation. Work with ERP/finance systems teams to enhance reporting accuracy and turnaround time. Support implementation of tools like Power BI, Tableau, or advanced Excel models. 7. Stakeholder & Compliance Support Ensure accuracy and integrity of financial data used for decision-making. Key Skills:• Strong expertise in budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and financial modelling • Advanced proficiency in MS Excel (Power Query, Pivot, Macros preferred) • Exposure to Power BI / Tableau preferred • Strong understanding of P&L, Balance Sheet, Cashflow • Strong analytical thinking and business acumen • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills • Ability to work in fast-paced environments and manage multiple deadlines Preferred Qualifications: • CA / CMA / MBA Finance preferred • Strong experience in GCC / Real Estate / Construction / Infrastructure / Corporate Finance is an advantage • Experience working with ERP systems like SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Dynamics, etc.

Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose.Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege.About The Finance Planning & Reporting CoEOur Finance team is not just another department; we're strategic partners and catalysts for change, who use our expertise to drive sustainable growth and create and protect value for Reckitt.With a strong emphasis on cross-functional collaboration and partnership, our inclusive, diverse, and energised team breaks down traditional barriers, enabling you to thrive in a dynamic environment where you and your ideas matter.From leveraging insights and analysis to make data-driven decisions - to spearheading sustainable business practices that contribute to consistent and reliable business growth - we are unwaveringly passionate about making a meaningful impact and always doing the right thing.About The RoleAs Centre of Excellence (CoE) & Planning and Reporting (P&R) Lead, you will play a pivotal role in our Finance Planning and Analysis (FP&A) team, reporting directly to the VP of FP&A. You’ll provide thought leadership and drive continuous improvement across all planning and reporting activities, ensuring FP&A operates efficiently and delivers high-quality, trusted outputs.You’ll lead the P&R CoE to deliver best-in-class planning, forecasting, and reporting capabilities while identify opportunities to optimize FP&A processes and enhance how the FP&A team operates. By combining a deep understanding of data, process, and business context, you will transform how FP&A delivers insights and supports strategic decision making.Your responsibilitiesStrategic Leadership: Lead the strategic vision and direction of the CoE and P&R initiatives, championing continuous improvement and clarity of purpose across FP&A. Collaboration with Finance Leadership: Partner closely with Area CFOs, CHQ Leadership, and other hub leads to align planning and reporting strategy and enable efficient, coordinated hub delivery. Operational Management: Oversee the central operational execution of all baseline planning, forecasting, and reporting activities, ensuring high-quality deliverables that drive business insights. Financial Analytics Framework: Implement a framework for reporting and analytics that supports data-driven decision-making, including the development of standardized reporting processes and automation of forecasting models. Global Standards & Process Improvement: Champion continuous improvement by leading initiatives to standardize and enhance FP&A processes, promote best practices, and enhance team efficiency. Performance Monitoring: Oversee key performance indicators, ensuring accurate planning and reporting that provides actionable insights to leadership. Finance Transformation: Participate in finance transformation initiatives by championing opportunities and technology solutions to streamline and improve existing FP&A processes, standardisation of report templates and the automation of manual tasks to enhance efficiency. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Ability to work collaboratively across geographies and functions Experienced in managing teams and driving operational excellence and continuous improvement Thought leadership with FP&A tools, Excel, and reporting/visualization platforms (e.g., Power BI). Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities, with a strategic mindset to align technology solutions with business goals. Strong communication skills, capable of bridging the gap between finance and business stakeholders. LtdBiological E. Limited (BE), founded in 1953, is India’s first private-sector biological products company and the first pharmaceutical company in South India. Originally incorporated as Biological Products Private Limited, BE develops, manufactures, and supplies vaccines and therapeutics.BE’s portfolio includes 12 World Health Organization (WHO)-approved vaccines and 11 USFDA-approved generic injectables. The company has the capability to develop and manufacture vaccines across all major technology platforms, including microbial/bacterial fermentation, recombinant vaccines, and viral vaccines. BE supplies essential and life-saving vaccines and pharmaceuticals to UN agencies such as UNICEF and PAHO, global markets, and within India to the National Immunisation Programme, State Governments, Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs), the Indian Armed Forces, and the domestic retail market.For over five decades, BE has played a pivotal role in supporting public health programs in India. BE is the leading global supplier of Pentavalent vaccine and the largest producer of Tetanus vaccine worldwide. In India, it is the top producer of snake anti-venom and a prominent supplier of essential injectables, such as anti-coagulants. Additionally, BE offers trusted remedies for cough, cold, gastrointestinal disorders, respiratory issues, and critical care products.In recent years, BE has undertaken strategic initiatives for organizational expansion, including developing generic injectable products for regulated markets, exploring synthetic biology and metabolic engineering for sustainable API manufacturing, and creating novel vaccines for global markets.BE exports vaccines to over 140 countries, partnering with organizations' such as the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. The company has delivered several million doses of LPV to children worldwide and distributed over two billion doses of vaccines to public and private markets globally. job_title: Manager - Cost - 成本经理 - Hilton Shanghai Fengxian
job_description: With thousands of hotels in over 100 countries and territories, Hilton offers countless opportunities to delight. From an open door to a welcoming smile and an exceptional experience, we offer the millions of travellers who stay with us every year a welcome they will never forget. In addition to our flagship brand, Hilton Hotels & Resorts, the family of brands includes Waldorf Astoria, LXR, Conrad, Canopy, Curio Collection, DoubleTree, Hilton Garden Inn, Hampton and many others.If you appreciate the impact global travel can have on the world, you may be just the person we are looking for to work as a Hilton Team Member. Because it’s with Hilton where we never forget the reason we're here: to delight our guests, Team Members, and owners alike.The core responsibilities of the Manager - Cost are to ensure the smooth and efficient operation of cost control, storerooms, receiving and assist management in keeping the cost of food, beverage and other supplies to a minimum.What will I be doing?As the Manager - Cost, you will be responsible for performing the following tasks to the highest standards: Supervise Cost Clerks, Storekeepers, and Receiving Clerks. Ensure that all incoming goods are received via the receiving area, with deliveries properly checked against purchase orders and market lists. Ensure the security of all stores, including proper restrictions on the issue of keys and general access. Record and verify on a daily basis all food and beverage invoices for price extension, total and unit prices which should conform to the approved market prices. Ensure all inventory items are correctly set up in the hotel inventory system including sizes, descriptions, purchase units etc. Ensure that proper records are kept of inventory receipts, issues, returns and transfers and proper authorization thereof. Inspect and test goods whenever necessary especially with regard to food and beverage items. Ensure that accumulated daily invoices agree to amounts transferred to Accounts Payable. Make regular spot checks and inspections of storerooms, receiving area, and all outlets. Check duty invoices to ensure that correct duty has been applied. Have full and extensive knowledge of the hotel inventory system and is responsible for the maintenance of the system. Should there any system error detected, bring attention to the DOF / AFC immediately. 财务经理业务部门：运营财务工作地点-国家/地区：中国，上海职位介绍：我们正在寻求拥有专业和管理经验的候选人，充满好奇心并愿意学习和成长，拥有突出的分析技能和卓越的学术成绩。在这个职位上，您将带领业务线或特定业务领域的财务联络人团队，积极影响业务团队有效执行预算，使公司得以实现业绩目标；并为各类关键业务决策提供财务分析的独到见解，推动公司业务的可持续增长。您将有机会接触上海迪士尼度假区多个业务线的工作内容，发挥思考型伙伴的作用，与业务部门和团队成员协同合作，推进各类跨部门项目，使公司从长远的角度得以高效地运用资源和实现发展。工作内容：您将作为业务部门的财务伙伴、支持该业务线的日常运作，包括但不限于处理运营中出现的财务类需求、协调会计账务处理、预算与支出的相关审批、协调多部门沟通等；您将支持公司各关键业务周期的财务计划：长期经营计划、全年经营预算和短期滚动预测，为业务部门有效完成预算目标提供持续的建议和方案，并确保与公司长期战略一致；您将持续跟进分析业务部门的业绩表现，更新并持续改进常规业绩分析报告，提出有价值的见解并提出建议方案，并在各类会议中进行报告演讲；您将深度参与各类研究分析项目（如特定业绩表现分析、投资回报分析、商业策略和业务合作的财务模型测算、 成本效率提升、商业机会挖掘等），独立建立和维护财务分析模型，以清晰，简洁和结构化的方式撰写分析报告，为复杂的关键业务决策提供策略性、有价值的见解，实现公司业绩增值。我们希望您：具备MBA或硕士学历并拥有外企相关财务管理领域5-7年工作经验，或者本科学位并拥有外企相关财务管理领域7-10年工作经验在项目财务、运营财务、财务/商业计划分析、 财务报告、财务管理与控制和会计方面有丰富的工作经验熟练掌握定量分析和财务建模，逻辑思维能力强，具有问题解决和关键性思维技能具备突出的组织技能和团队合作能力，注重大局也能关注细节良好的书面和口头沟通技能，以简洁积极的方式传达关键信息自我激励，能够处理复杂情况，能胜任在一家跨国企业多元化的经营活动中工作具备高度的责任感，可以同时处理多项任务熟练使用微软办公自动化软件 (如：Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook)，具备SAP或其它财务/ERP系统经验，能够快速学习和运用新技能/系统中英文口头和书面均流利以身作则领导团队，具有领导并发展团队的能力对娱乐业和旅游业充满热情，热爱迪士尼品牌关于上海迪士尼度假区：作为全球家庭娱乐业的领军企业之一，上海迪士尼度假区为我们的游客带来独具一格的服务和珍藏一生的记忆。加入我们，在这个充满奇幻、想象、创意和探险的全新世界里，为每年数以百万的度假区游客创造欢乐。无论您的迪士尼旅程走向台前还是幕后，您都拥有无限机遇。 我们致力于提供平等的就业机会。 具体的福利基于职位有所不同，可能根据公司政策进行调整。 上海迪士尼度假区不会委托任何人或第三方机构向求职者收取费用。候选人请务必通过度假区官方招聘渠道获取度假区招聘的相关资讯。Finance ManagerLine of Business: Operations FinanceLocation: Shanghai, ChinaJob Summary：We are seeking candidates with accomplished professional experience, curiosity, and desire to learn and grow, demonstrated analytical skills and outstanding academic records. In this role, you will lead a team that consists of finance partners to line(s) of business or specific business area and proactively influence the business team to execute budgets effectively so that the company can achieve its business results; and provide impeccable insights through financial analysis to inform various key business decisions and drive sustainable growth for the company.You will have the opportunity to get exposed to a wide variety of lines of business at Shanghai Disney Resort, and play the role of a thought partner, to collaborate with business departments and team members on various cross-departmental projects, so that the resources are effectively and efficiently utilized to drive the growth from the lens of long-term success.Key Responsibilities：You will perform as the finance business partner to support the operations of line(s) of business or specific business area as the key contact person, including but not limited to handling the finance related questions, coordinating accounting journal entries, reviewing budget and funding related requests, as well as facilitating cross-functional discussions for finance related matters;You will support the making of company's financial plans across key business cycles: Long Term Plan, Annual Operating Plan and rolling forecasting cycles, provide suggestions and action plans to line of business to manage the budget and meet the targets effectively in alignment with the strategic plan;You will continuously analyze the business performance of the line of business; improve and deliver regular management report on business performance while provide valuable insights and recommendations on business solution alternatives, and make a presentation at various business meetings;You will be deeply involved in various analytical projects (eg. ad hoc performance review, return on investment analysis, financial modeling behind the commercial strategy and business deal, cost efficiency improvement, business opportunity assessment, etc.), to build up financial models independently; write analysis reports in a clear, concise, and well-structured manner; provide strategic and value-added insights to support key decisions for complex business and contribute to company’s business growth.Required Qualifications：MBA or other Master’s degree in the related management finance area of 5-7 years, or Bachelor’s degree and 7-10 years relevant management financeAbundant experience in project finance, operations finance, financial planning, business planning or development, financial and/or management reporting, accounting, and/or corporate financeAdvanced proficiency in quantitative analysis, problem-solving, and financial modelingDemonstrated organizational skills and teamwork spirit with big picture and attention to detailRefined written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to convey key elements with a concise and positive mannerSelf-motivated and can deal with complexity, ability to work in a diversified business activities of a multinational companyStrong sense of responsibility and the ability to meet deadlinesStrong computer skills (e.g. Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook), with knowledge of financial systems (e.g. SAP) and software with the ability to quickly learn and apply new skills and techniquesFluent in spoken and written Chinese and EnglishLeadership by example, ability to lead and develop a strong team of finance professionalsEnthusiasm for th

job_title: 财务经理（能接受1-2年后外派出国）
job_description: 职位来源于智联招聘。此岗位近1-2年工作地点为南京市江宁区将军大道527号南京寒锐钴业股份有限公司1-2年后需外派非洲刚果金或印尼，外派时间期限为3年，3年后可申请调回国内工作，国外年薪为税后40-50万岗位职责：1、组织实施基地核算，编制会计报表；为管理部门提供全面、准确的财务数据信息；2、组织搭建基地财务制度体系及流程梳理，保证公司制度完善修订及财、物流程优化效率，保障业务有效运行；3、组织基地开展年度全面预算编制、定期组织分析公司重大项目或资产投入预算执行情况，分析差异原因并提出管理建议；4、组织基地原材料价格、生产制程、仓储物流全过程的成本管理；5、组织搭建基地财务核算系统建设，保障系统正常运行并不断优化；6、组织审核、主动流动资金的使用，进行项目融资工作，确保公司资金安全及有效利用。任职要求：1、本科及以上学历，财务等相关专业；2、作为公司财务部门负责人，独立操作过中等企业产研销一体化公司的财务管理经验；3、有制造业成本管理工作经验；4、持有中级会计师及以上职称证书；5、高度责任心、执行力和组织归属感。以担保或任何理由索要财物，扣押证照，均涉嫌违法。

该职位来源于猎聘 岗位职责 总包项目财务管理：全面负责总包项目的财务全周期管理（含预算编制、成本控制、资金计划、竣工结算），主导过至少3个20亿+体量项目的总包财务工作，熟悉EPC、PC等模式下的财务管控要点。 成本动态管控：深度参与总包合同谈判及变更审核，建立动态成本台账，实时监控建安成本偏差（需熟悉《建设工程工程量清单计价规范》），对超支风险提出预警及解决方案。 资金与融资协同：根据总包施工节点制定资金计划，协调总包单位与集团资金池的对接，熟悉总包代采、保理、供应链ABS等融资工具，降低资金占用成本。 税务筹划与清算：主导总包项目土增税清算，熟悉总包成本分摊规则（如建筑面积法、直接成本法），通过合理分摊降低税负，有成功清算案例者优先。 任职要求： 5年以上房地产财务经验，其中总包板块财务负责人经验≥3年，熟悉总包招投标、变更签证、竣工结算全流程； 主导过至少1个项目从开工到清算的全周期财务管理，需提供案例（项目体量≥20亿）； 精通总包成本科目（如建安工程费、措施费、分包成本），能快速识别成本异常（如总包虚报工程量、材料价差）；

job_title: Administrative & Operations Assistant, LSC-5, Bridgetown, Barbados
job_description: Job DescriptionHow you can make a difference:UNFPA is the lead United Nations agency for delivering a world where every pregnancy is intended, every childbirth is safe, and every young person's potential is fulfilled. The UNFPA Strategic Plan for 2026-2029 articulates the organization’s response to a complex global environment, providing a roadmap for resilience and renewal. It is designed to accelerate the implementation of the Programme of Action of the International Conference on Population and Development (ICPD) and the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals by 2030. This mandate is pursued through a focus on four interconnected outcomes: ending the unmet need for family planning; ending preventable maternal deaths; ending gender-based violence and harmful practices; and adapting to demographic change through evidence and rights-based policies.In a world where fundamental human rights are at risk, we need principled and ethical staff, who embody these international norms and standards and who will defend them courageously and with full conviction.UNFPA is seeking candidates who transform, inspire, and deliver high-impact and sustained results and ensure effective external relations, communications, and partnership-building and resource mobilization in a rapidly changing development and funding landscape. We need staff who are transparent, exceptional in how they manage the resources entrusted to them, and who commit to delivering excellence in programme results.Job Purpose:The Administrative & Operations Assistant will provide support to ensure that the business processes are followed in the processing of financial transactions related to office management and operations. He/she is instrumental in facilitating administrative support and office management/operations using and developing appropriate mechanisms and systems - particularly Quantum ERP- and ensuring compliance with established procedures.The incumbent will furthermore implement and support the adherence and monitoring of UNFPA internal remote audit recommendations, and has the responsibility to safekeep and reconcile assets.You would be responsible for:Contributes to the substantive programmatic and logistical support for UNFPA programmatic interventions, workshops, capacity building activities, commemoration ofInternational Days, review meetings with stakeholders, advocacy and public information events, and other initiatives, including those within the joint UN framework.Support the timely and efficient support to office financial and administration tasks including payment of vouchers, bills, travel tickets, per diem for meeting participants,consultancies, procurement of goods and services and other related processes.Contributes to the smooth running of the office by ensuring the provision and maintenance of services and supplies, maintaining up-to-date inventory and records andthe appropriate common premises related activities. Ensure business continuation in accordance with approved AWP, through program and financial assistance in accordancewith PPM. Implement and provide support for adherence, monitoring and follow up of audit recommendations.Works in close collaboration with SROC, implementing and cooperating with UN, government, civil society partners, relevant humanitarian agencies, stakeholders and fieldstaff to coordinate logistics and supply chain management support for UNFPA operations including in emergencies.Ensures timely and effective procurement and management of UNFPA commodities and supplies at all levels, including delivery, transportation, storage, distribution, inventory and reporting of all UNFPA goods. Undertakes field monitoring visits to warehouses to ensure proper storage, distribution and utilization of emergency supplies.Supports the establishment of IP agreements with partners for implementation of UNFPA work plans and LTAs. Provides support in projecting future needs (forecasting) forprepositioning supplies and identifying gaps in the logistics pipeline, providing inputs on how to strengthen the logistics chain. Additionally, supports the management of cash transfers and reporting by Implementing Partners.Assists in capacity building, training and guiding of UNFPA stakeholders, including on the use of QuantumERP in managing budgets and generating reports and PSEAobligations.Liaises with stakeholders in the SROC in the fulfillment of daily administrative tasks and to advance the programme and mandate of UNFPA including with governments, regional institutions, civil society organizations including but not limited to FBOs, female and youth-led organizations and others. Provision of support to UNFPA Offices in other Caribbean countries is expected as well.Assist with managing cash transfers to Implementing Partners and timely reporting of advances, capacity building of IP on financial, managementAssist with HACT Assurances activities of implementing Partner and required follow up actions from the assurance

job_description: DescriptionAmazon is looking for a driven Finance Operations Specialist to become a key contributor in the fast-paced world of Global Accounts Payable. This role offers an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a high-growth environment, where your ability to identify, communicate, and drive operational improvements will be critical to the success of our team. You will have ownership of a critical reporting function, manage relationships with multiple global stakeholders, and lead problem-solving initiatives to ensure timely payments to vendors.We are seeking individuals with excellent communication and analytical skills, combined with a strong passion for process improvement and problem-solving. If you are motivated to dive into the details, take full ownership, and work effectively in a fast-paced environment, we have the perfect career opportunity for you!The ideal candidate will have experience in finance roles, with a deep understanding of finance operations and a solid background in relationship management. The candidate will demonstrate excellent problem-solving abilities, with a focus on driving process improvements and enhancing payment practices. We are looking for an action-driven individual who is motivated to proactively address challenges, identify areas for improvement, and implement solutions to enhance overall efficiency. About IntouchCXIntouchCX is a global leader in customer experience management, digital engagement, and AI and automation solutions. We immerse ourselves in your world with curiosity, creativity, and innovation to deliver exceptional results. For over 20 years, we have scaled with soul to become a disruptive industry leader by building trusted long-term relationships with our brand partners and empowering our people to drive positive change.About The JobWe are looking for a highly detailed and organized Junior Analyst to join our dynamic Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team. This role is critical to the accuracy of our billing process. The successful candidate will be responsible for auditing agent hours tracked across our systems for over 200 clients, ensuring data integrity and preventing revenue leakage. This position offers an excellent opportunity to develop analytical skills within a fast-paced call center environment.As Junior Analyst, FP&A, You Will… Perform daily audits of agent hours logged in our tracking systems against the scheduled time to ensure accuracy for billing purposes. Systematically identify, investigate, and document variances, discrepancies, and potential errors in reported hours. Collaborate proactively with Operations leadership to facilitate the timely investigation and correction of identified discrepancies. Maintain detailed logs of audit findings and track the status of resolutions to ensure all issues are brought to a close. Escalate persistent issues or significant discrepancies to management clearly and concisely to ensure prompt resolution. Assist in generating reports related to agent productivity, billable hours, and audit outcomes. Contribute to the continuous improvement of audit processes and best practices.As Junior Analyst, FP&A, You Need… Post-secondary education in Business, Finance, Accounting, or a related field is preferred. Proven proficiency in Google Sheets / Microsoft Excel is essential. Exceptional attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy are paramount. Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to manage a high volume of tasks and meet daily deadlines. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex issues clearly to various stakeholders. A proactive and persistent approach to problem-solving and follow-up. Ability to work independently as well as part of a collaborative team. Previous experience in an analyst, auditing, or call center role is an asset but not required.

company_name: D. E. Shaw India Private Limited
job_title: Analyst – Asset Backed Strategies (CLO and Credit Derivatives) - Financial Research
job_description: The D. E. Shaw group is a global investment and technology development firm with more than $65 billion in investment capital as of March 1, 2025, and offices in North America, Europe, and Asia. Since our founding in 1988, our firm has earned an international reputation for successful investing based on innovation, careful risk management, and the quality and depth of our staff. We have a significant presence in the world's capital markets, investing in a wide range of companies and financial instruments in both developed and developing economies.We are looking for a candidate to join our Asset Backed Strategies (ABS) team within our Financial Research group based in Bengaluru and Hyderabad, with a focus on liquid credit/collateralized loan obligations. The group works closely with the Global ABS front office to provide in-depth analytical support to the investment research and decision-making processes of the firm’s proprietary investment activity in the ABS markets.WHAT YOU'LL DO DAY-TO-DAY:In this role, you will support the senior analysts in India and the front office in researching and analyzing US and European credit markets, with emphasis on CLOs and credit derivatives. You will perform sector and thematic research and monitor market developments, as well as evaluate single-name credit opportunities in liquid credit markets. You will provide macroeconomic updates, quantitative analyses, and data-driven insights to the trading desk. You will also analyze CLO structures, including tranche performance, collateral quality, and cash flow behavior using bespoke and vendor models. Additionally, you will conduct credit analysis of CLOs in both primary and secondary markets, interpret deal documentation, and assess covenant implications. You will monitor and evaluate existing CLO and credit derivative portfolios to deliver timely recommendations based on performance and market events. Finally, you will work extensively with large datasets, research tools, and vendor platforms to generate research outputs and enhance portfolio surveillance.WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:Basic qualifications:A degree in finance or equivalent (Master of Business Administration/Chartered Financial Analyst) along with 1 to 3 years of experienceSolid knowledge of fixed income research, with expertise in either structured credit / credit derivatives or the corporate credit / CLO spaceExcellent technical abilities in Python and SQLProven track record of analyzing complex datasets and applying statistical models to derive insightsExperience with working independently as well as collaboratively within a teamPreferred qualifications:Experience working with a fixed income strategy, either sell side or buy sideInterested candidates can apply through our website: https://www.deshawindia.com/recruit/jobs/Adv/Link/AnlAsBackCLOJul25We encourage candidates with relevant experience looking to restart their careers after a break to apply for this position. Learn about Recommence, our gender-neutral return-to-work initiative.The Firm offers excellent benefits, a casual, collegial working environment, and an attractive compensation package. For further information about our recruitment process, including how applicant data will be processed, please visit https://www.deshawindia.com/careersMembers of the D. E. Shaw group do not discriminate in employment matters on the basis of sex, race, colour, caste, creed, religion, pregnancy, national origin, age, military service eligibility, veteran status, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, or any other protected class.

In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc. Strong organizational skills with an inquisitive mindset. Advanced math skills. Ability to calculate complicated figures such as percentages, fractions, and other financial-related calculations. job_title: AWS Finance analyst I, AWS Finance
job_description: DescriptionAmazon is seeking a skilled Finance Operations Specialist to join our AWS Finance Operations team. This role focuses on managing global asset transfers, developing key performance metrics, and optimizing financial processes across AWS operations. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong problem-solving capabilities with a commitment to continuous process improvement and exceptional communication skills across all levels of the organization.Key job responsibilitiesWe are seeking an experienced and results-oriented analyst to join the Accounts Payable team within AWS Finops. This is a individual contributor role designed for a finance operations professional who brings deep expertise in procure-to-pay (P2P) processes specifically on clearing on hold invoices and possesses a proven track record of success in complex, fast-paced environments. The ideal candidate will be someone who thrives in a dynamic, global setting where priorities shift rapidly, and cross-functional collaboration is essential.Finance Operations & Process Excellence Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify process gaps, root causes, and improvement opportunities. Drive daily operations to meet or exceed performance, accuracy, and controllership targets lead resolution of critical vendor issues and ensure corrective actions are implemented to enhance customer experience. Represent Finance Operations in leadership reviews and stakeholder discussionsStrategy, Risk & Continuous Improvement. Contribute to short-, mid-, and long-term operational improvement roadmapsIdentify and assess financial and operational risks and opportunities, driving mitigation plans and ad-hoc initiatives Use data-driven insights to influence decisions, improve efficiency, and support business growth. Champion continuous improvement initiatives that strengthen automation, accuracy, and scalability.A day in the lifeCollaborate with multiple vendors and stakeholders to resolve invoice holds and ensure timely payment processing, maintaining strong relationships to facilitate smooth payment operations.Perform regular reconciliation of vendor statements to ensure accuracy and alignment between internal records and supplier reports, proactively identifying and resolving discrepancies.Maintain and update the monthly business review document, incorporating feedback from stakeholders to ensure comprehensive reporting and continuous process improvement.About The TeamThe One Pay Function (OPF) team was established in April 2020 to streamline payment operations for AWS rack and Amazon Funded Buy-Sell (AFBS) suppliers on a global scale.Core Responsibilities. The team serves as a central hub for payment coordination, working directly with ODM vendors to: Obtain open invoice receivables statements Reconcile these statements against AWS payables Coordinate with multiple stakeholders to ensure proper invoice processing and paymentStakeholder CollaborationBasic Qualifications Bachelor's degree or above in finance, accounting or related fieldPreferred Qualifications 6+ years of Accounts Payable (AP) experienceOur inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. job_title: 财务计划分析专员/Financial Planning Analyst
job_description: Job DescriptionChange the world. Love your job.In your first year with TI, you will participate in the Career Accelerator Program (CAP), which provides professional and technical training and resources to accelerate your ramp into TI, and set you up for long-term career success. Within this program, we also offer function-specific technical training and on-the-job learning opportunities that will encourage you to solve problems through a variety of hands-on, meaningful experiences from your very first day on the job.In a Financial Analyst role you will provide leadership in the areas of financial forecasting, efficiency improvements, reporting enhancements, risk and profitability analyses, and financial modeling. Your role may be in one or more of these areas: Manufacturing, Business Unit, Financial PlanningResponsibilities IncludeDriving financial excellence and ensuring the accuracy and integrity of the financial results for the businessCross functional partnering to ensure financial complianceUnderstanding and explaining the relationship between operational performance, financial results and complianceProviding a strategic view of the business and processes by incorporating competitive analysis to drive financial performanceContinuous process improvement and automation in financial processes and reporting activitiesPut your talent to work with Texas Instruments!QualificationsMinimum requirements:Bachelor's degree in finance or related business degreeCumulative 3.0/4.0 GPA or higher Fresh graduates or graduates with less than 2 years working experience Preferred QualificationsStrong understanding of internal control principles and accounting policies and proceduresSystems knowledge/skills including: Excel, SAPStrong analytical and problem-solving skillsStrong written and verbal communication skillsAbility to team and collaborate effectively with people in different functions, countries and culturesAbility to build strong, influential relationshipsAbility to take initiative, prioritize work, balance multiple projects and drive for results in a fast-paced and rapidly changing environmentNavigates ambiguityAttention to detailAbout UsWhy TI?Engineer your future. We empower our employees to truly own their career and development. Come collaborate with some of the smartest people in the world to shape the future of electronics.We're different by design. Diverse backgrounds and perspectives are what push innovation forward and what make TI stronger. We value each and every voice, and look forward to hearing yours. Meet the people of TI Benefits that benefit you. We offer competitive pay and benefits designed to help you and your family live your best life. Your well-being is important to us.About Texas InstrumentsTexas Instruments Incorporated (Nasdaq: TXN) is a global semiconductor company that designs, manufactures and sells analog and embedded processing chips for markets such as industrial, automotive, data center, personal electronics and communications equipment. At our core, we have a passion to create a better world by making electronics more affordable through semiconductors. This passion is alive today as each generation of innovation builds upon the last to make our technology more reliable, more affordable and lower power, making it possible for semiconductors to go into electronics everywhere. Learn more at TI.com .Texas Instruments is an equal opportunity employer and supports a diverse, inclusive work environment.If you are interested in this position, please apply to this requisition.

job_title: Asset Management - Convertible Bond Research Analyst - Shanghai
job_description: Job DescriptionResearch is fast-paced, dynamic, and intellectually stimulating.As an Analyst in Fixed Income Research, you will help to conduct and facilitate industry analysis and company-specific coverage. This involves core research ranging from dissecting earnings releases and regulatory filings to assessing relevant news events, macro trends, modelling financial statements and industry dynamics, performing detailed valuation work, writing research notes and sector pieces and making investment recommendations.Job ResponsibilitiesFollow specific sectors and companies in China onshore convertible bond marketProvide timely analysis and recommendation on covered companies and sectorsFormulate sector strategies and investment recommendationsEffectively communicate thoughts, ideas and analysis in written and oral formMaintaining regular contact with Global Fixed Income, Currency and Commodities (GFICC) colleagues globally, taking part in conference calls and responding to project requestsRequired Qualifications, Capabilities, And SkillsPast experience in China onshore convertible bond research/investment roleAttainment of all necessary regulatory licenses (or any other licenses / qualifications as required) for carrying out Asset Management and other regulated activitiesDemonstrate a strong ability to address risk and be comfortable with risk-takingStrong written and oral communication skillsStrong financial analytical skills Ability to work effectively with colleagues based in around the worldPreferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And SkillsProficiency in EnglishABOUT USJ.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first-class business in a first-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.About The TeamJ.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management delivers industry-leading investment management and private banking solutions. Asset Management provides individuals, advisors and institutions with strategies and expertise that span the full spectrum of asset classes through our global network of investment professionals. Wealth Management helps individuals, families and foundations take a more intentional approach to their wealth or finances to better define, focus and realize their goals.
